senses.definition: toward the side and away from the main path

examples.x: He turned aside to avoid the obstacle in his path.
The cat jumped aside as the car approached.
He moved aside to make room for the incoming crowd.
The cat nudged the toy aside with its paw.

senses.definition: used to convey that something seems to be true based on the available evidence or information

examples.x: He apparently left the office early today; his desk is empty.
The car is parked in the driveway, so apparently, someone is home.
Apparently, the train is delayed due to technical issues.
She apparently forgot about our meeting, as she didn't show up on time.
The restaurant is apparently famous for its seafood dishes.

senses.definition: used to say that something such as a number or amount is not exact

examples.x: The journey will take approximately two hours, depending on traffic.
The population of the city is approximately 500,000 residents.
The meeting is scheduled to start at 3 PM, give or take approximately 15 minutes.
The package will arrive in approximately five business days.
The temperature is expected to reach approximately 25 degrees Celsius tomorrow.

senses.definition: in a simple or fundamental manner, without concern for less important details

examples.x: She explained the complex scientific concept in a way that anyone could understand, breaking it down to basically illustrate its core principles.
After hours of troubleshooting, the technician concluded that, basically, the problem was a faulty power supply.
The new software is basically an upgraded version of the previous one, with improved features and enhanced security.
In his speech, the professor essentially said that, basically, curiosity is the driving force behind scientific discovery.

senses.definition: in a way that is always the same

examples.x: He consistently delivers high-quality work on every project.
The athlete consistently performs well in competitions.
The company has consistently met its quarterly targets.
She consistently attends the weekly meetings without fail.
The weather in this region is consistently sunny during the summer.

senses.definition: in a way that continues without any pause

examples.x: She was constantly checking her phone for messages.
He was constantly striving to improve his skills.
The background music at the cafe played constantly.
She constantly reminded herself to stay focused on her goals.
The street was constantly busy with pedestrians and traffic.

senses.definition: in a manner that shows one's disapproval of someone or something

examples.x: She looked at the proposal critically, pointing out its weaknesses during the team meeting.
The reviewer assessed the film critically, expressing dissatisfaction with the plot and character development.
The manager reviewed the employee's performance critically, identifying areas for improvement.
He listened to the speaker critically, questioning the validity of the arguments presented.

senses.definition: in a way that shows courage

examples.x: The fashion designer daringly experimented with vibrant colors and unconventional fabrics.
The artist daringly challenged societal norms through provocative artwork.
She daringly quit her stable job to pursue her passion for travel photography.
She arrived at the party wearing a daringly short skirt that turned heads and sparked conversations throughout the evening.

senses.definition: to a great degree

examples.x: She felt deeply touched by the heartfelt gesture.
The apology was deeply sincere and meaningful.
The team was deeply committed to achieving their goals.
The loss affected him deeply, and he needed time to recover.
She deeply regrets not attending her best friend’s wedding.

senses.definition: in a manner that was planned and purposeful

examples.x: She deliberately chose not to respond to the provocation, maintaining her composure.
The speaker deliberately used humor to lighten the mood during the serious discussion.
The athlete deliberately practiced specific techniques to improve performance during competitions.
The chef deliberately selected fresh, locally sourced ingredients for the special menu.
He deliberately changed his route to avoid encountering his ex-partner.

senses.definition: in a manner that is not as one had hoped for

examples.x: The long-awaited sequel to the popular series was released, but it was disappointingly lacking in creativity.
The restaurant had great reviews, but the food was disappointingly bland.
Despite hours of preparation, the team's performance was disappointingly subpar.
The much-anticipated event was disappointingly short and uneventful.
The exam results were disappointingly lower than expected, causing frustration.

senses.definition: at, in, or to another place

examples.x: If you can't find what you're looking for here, you might find it elsewhere.
He searched for a job locally but decided to look elsewhere for better opportunities.
If the store doesn't have the book, you may find it elsewhere in the city.
If this restaurant is full, we can always try dining elsewhere.
When the local market didn't have the specific ingredients, she had to shop elsewhere for her recipe.

senses.definition: to the fullest or complete degree

examples.x: The puzzle was entirely solved by the dedicated team.
The situation was entirely out of my control.
The concept was entirely new to the scientific community.
The project was entirely funded by the government.
The room was entirely empty after the move.

senses.definition: in small amounts over a long period of time

examples.x: As the project advanced, the team's understanding of the requirements deepened gradually.
The colors of the sunset changed gradually from orange to pink.
His health improved gradually as he followed the prescribed treatment.
The temperature dropped gradually throughout the evening.
The student's confidence in public speaking grew gradually with practice.

senses.definition: in a manner that does not cause harm or danger

examples.x: The small wave crashed harmlessly against the shore.
The puppy gnawed harmlessly on a chew toy.
The children played harmlessly in the backyard, enjoying their games.
The electrical issue was resolved harmlessly by a professional.

senses.definition: in a way that shows there is no hope for success or improvement

examples.x: He was hopelessly lost in the unfamiliar city without a map.
They watched the game hopelessly as their team fell behind.
They were hopelessly trying to fix the broken car with no tools.
He searched hopelessly for a solution to the complex problem.

senses.definition: at the starting point of a process or situation

examples.x: The drug was initially tested on mice before human trials.
We initially planned to launch in June, but production delays pushed us to August.
I initially hated sushi, but now it's my favorite food.
The app initially had just 100 users; today it serves millions.
The treaty was initially signed by only three nations, though others later joined.

senses.definition: in a way that cannot be stopped or avoided, and certainly happens

examples.x: With the heavy rain, traffic jams inevitably ensued on the city streets.
As technology advances, certain jobs will inevitably be replaced by automation.
If you neglect regular maintenance, the car will inevitably experience mechanical issues.
In a rapidly changing world, cultural traditions inevitably evolve over time.
As the population grows, urban areas inevitably expand to accommodate the increasing demand for housing.

senses.definition: to a great extent

examples.x: The success of the project was largely due to efficient resource allocation.
The city's skyline is largely dominated by modern skyscrapers.
Her achievements in the field are largely recognized and celebrated.
The decision was largely influenced by economic factors and market trends.
The film's popularity is largely attributed to its compelling storyline and strong performances.

senses.definition: in a manner that expresses what is really intended, without exaggeration

examples.x: Her excitement was so intense that she literally jumped up and down when she received the award.
The comedian's jokes about his job were funny because they were literally true; he worked in a clown costume at a theme park.
The warning sign "Beware of Dog" should be taken literally; there is a large and protective guard dog on the premises.
She was so angry that she was literally shaking.

senses.definition: used to introduce an opposing statement

examples.x: The plan was risky; they proceeded nevertheless.
The data was flawed; the team published it nevertheless.
She doubted his motives; she trusted him nevertheless.
The cost was prohibitive; they bought it nevertheless.
The path was forbidden; they walked it nevertheless.

senses.definition: not on a regular basis

examples.x: She visits her grandparents occasionally.
He occasionally takes spontaneous road trips.
She reads novels, and occasionally, she writes short stories.
They occasionally attend live performances.
We meet for coffee occasionally.

senses.definition: used to refer to the outcome of a situation if the circumstances were different

examples.x: You should pack your umbrella, otherwise you might get wet in the rain.
He needs to study for the test, otherwise he might not pass.
Please handle the vase carefully, otherwise it could break.
She needs to leave now, otherwise she will miss her train.
Make sure to water the plants regularly, otherwise they may wilt.

senses.definition: with everyone or everything included

examples.x: He felt overall satisfied with his performance in the exam, despite a few mistakes.
The feedback on the proposal was overall positive, with some constructive criticism for improvement.
The company's financial performance was overall satisfactory, although there were fluctuations in certain quarters.
The event was overall enjoyable, with attendees expressing their satisfaction with the activities and entertainment provided.

senses.definition: to a specific extent or degree

examples.x: Her success in the competition was partly due to her dedication and partly due to natural talent.
The project's delay was partly caused by unforeseen circumstances beyond our control.
His decision to accept the offer was partly influenced by career growth opportunities.
The team's victory was partly a result of strategic planning and partly luck.
The novel's popularity can be attributed partly to its engaging plot and partly to the author's writing style.

senses.definition: in an exact way, often emphasizing correctness or clarity

examples.x: The coordinates were entered precisely to ensure accurate navigation.
She arrived at precisely 10 o’clock.
The clock is set to precisely match the atomic time standard.
This is precisely why we need to be careful.
The plan is precisely what we agreed on.
It's precisely the reason I didn't tell anyone.
That's precisely what I was trying to explain.
They arrived precisely on time for the meeting.

senses.definition: to a specific degree, particularly when compared to other similar things

examples.x: The city is relatively small compared to the bustling metropolis nearby.
Her achievements in the field are relatively significant for someone of her age.
The new software is relatively easy to use, even for those without technical expertise.
The prices of goods in this area are relatively lower than in the city center.
The project was completed relatively quickly, thanks to efficient planning and execution.

senses.definition: in a way that demands or requires complete obedience

examples.x: Employees are strictly prohibited from using personal devices during work hours.
The school enforces a strictly enforced dress code to maintain a professional environment.
The security guards are instructed to strictly monitor access to restricted areas.
The contract terms must be strictly adhered to in order to avoid legal consequences.

senses.definition: to a moderate degree or extent

examples.x: The weather is somewhat cloudy, but rain is not expected.
After the revisions, the report is somewhat clearer, but more adjustments are needed.
His explanation was somewhat confusing, and I need further clarification.
The team's performance was somewhat satisfactory, but there is room for improvement.
The new policy has somewhat addressed the issue, but additional measures may be necessary.

senses.definition: after a particular event or time

examples.x: She moved to France and subsequently began studying art.
The plane landed safely and subsequently taxied to the gate.
She gave a brief introduction and subsequently opened the floor to questions.
He graduated in 2019 and subsequently moved to New York.
We visited the museum in the morning and subsequently had lunch by the river.

senses.definition: used for expressing the sincerity or honesty of a particular feeling, statement, etc.

examples.x: It is truly essential to listen to others to build strong relationships.
She truly loves her job and feels passionate about her work.
I truly appreciate your help with my project.
They were truly amazed by the beauty of the landscape.

senses.definition: after doing or considering everything

examples.x: After considering various options, they ultimately chose the most cost-effective solution for the project.
Despite facing challenges along the way, they ultimately succeeded in launching the product.
The goal of the research was to understand the underlying causes, and ultimately, to find effective solutions.
He pursued different career paths, but ultimately, he found fulfillment in entrepreneurship.
The team explored multiple strategies, and ultimately, they implemented the one with the greatest impact.

senses.definition: in a manner that does not achieve the desired outcome

examples.x: Despite multiple attempts, he unsuccessfully tried to repair the broken machinery.
She tried unsuccessfully to convince her parents to let her stay out later.
The negotiation between the two parties ended unsuccessfully, with no agreement reached.
The experiment was conducted unsuccessfully, yielding inconclusive results and no significant findings.

senses.definition: used to emphasize the amount or intensity of something

examples.x: He runs way faster than anyone else on the team.
The meeting ran way longer than expected.
She's way ahead of her classmates in math.
That movie was way better than the one we saw last week.
She's way too tired to go out tonight.

senses.definition: to a small amount or extent

examples.x: He's a tad taller than his older brother.
The dress was a tad too big, so she had it altered.
The meeting ran a tad longer than expected, but we covered all the agenda items.
She was feeling a tad tired after staying up late last night.
He adjusted the thermostat a tad higher to warm up the room.

senses.definition: to a large extent or degree

examples.x: Standards vary widely in different regions.
Opinions on the subject differ widely.
The two men have widely different views on the question.
There are widely divergent opinions about who is responsible.
The quality of the products varies widely.

senses.definition: in a caring and soft manner

examples.x: She spoke gently to the upset child, offering comfort.
The instructor corrected the mistake gently, encouraging the student to learn from it.
The cat approached the newborn kittens gently, displaying maternal care and affection.
The therapist spoke gently during the counseling session, creating a safe and supportive environment.

senses.definition: close or next to the side of something or someone
in a manner that is appropriate to the circumstances

examples.x: The weather forecast predicted rain, so she dressed accordingly with a raincoat and boots.
The team reviewed the feedback and adjusted their strategy accordingly.
The report highlighted areas for improvement, and the team acted accordingly to address them.
She adjusted her schedule accordingly to accommodate the unexpected meeting.

senses.definition: to or at the side that is further
